task: I’m trying to post a used item online and want to avoid making the listing look sloppy. Can you help me tighten up the title, photos, and message I should send if someone asks for a pickup time?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — the goal is to look clear, trustworthy, and easy to buy from.

### Title
Keep it simple and specific:
**Brand + model + key specs + condition**
- Example: **iPhone 13 Pro Max 256GB - Good Condition - Unlocked**
- If relevant: **Samsung Galaxy S22 128GB - Clean IMEI - No Cracks**

Avoid:
- “Must sell today”
- “Read description”
- Too many emojis or caps

### Photos
Use 4–6 clean photos:
1. Front
2. Back
3. Sides
4. Close-up of any scratches/damage
5. Screen on, if it powers up
6. Accessories included

Tips:
- Use daylight or bright indoor light
- Plain background
- No clutter
- Don’t hide flaws — show them clearly
- If it’s a phone, include a photo of the model/storage screen if possible

### Pickup message
Keep it short, polite, and specific.

**Example:**
> Hi, thanks for reaching out. I’m available for pickup today between 4–7 PM near [location]. If that works, send me a time that’s best for you.

If you want to sound a little firmer:
> Hi — I can do pickup today between 4–7 PM near [location]. Let me know what time you’d like to come by.

### Good habits
- State whether it’s cash only, Venmo, etc.
- Mention if the item is available until marked sold
- Don’t over-explain
- Reply fast and keep the tone calm

If you want, I can also help you write a clean **full listing description** in one paragraph.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is fine, but make it more usable.

I want:
- 3 title options, not just one
- a short photo checklist that sounds normal, not like a sales class
- 2 pickup texts: one chill, one a little more firm

Keep it tight. No extra lecture.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a tighter version.

### Title options
1. **[Brand] [Model] [Storage] - [Condition]**
2. **[Brand] [Model] - Clean / Working / [Key detail]**
3. **[Item] - [Condition] - [Main feature]**

Example:
- **iPhone 13 Pro Max 256GB - Good Condition**
- **Samsung S22 - Unlocked - Works Great**
- **Sony Headphones - Lightly Used - Includes Case**

### Photo checklist
- Front
- Back
- Sides
- Any scratches or wear
- Screen on / powered on if it has one
- Anything included

Keep the background clean and use natural light if you can.

### Pickup texts

**Chill:**
> Hey, thanks for reaching out. I’m free for pickup today between [time] and [time] near [location]. Let me know what works for you.

**More firm:**
> Hi — I can do pickup today between [time] and [time] near [location]. If that works, send me a time and I’ll hold it for you.

If you want, I can turn this into a fill-in-the-blank template.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, better. Still a little generic though.

Give me the same thing but more like a real marketplace post, not template-y. Like actual wording I can copy and change fast.

And make the pickup texts sound less robotic. The firm one especially sounds kinda fake.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s a more natural version you can actually copy and tweak fast.

### Title options
- **iPhone 13 Pro Max 256GB, good condition**
- **Samsung S22, unlocked, works great**
- **Sony headphones, lightly used, case included**

### Photo checklist
- Front
- Back
- Close-up of any wear
- Screen on if it has one
- Everything included in the deal

### Pickup texts

**Chill:**
> Hey, thanks for reaching out. I’m around today between 4 and 7 near [location] if you want to pick it up.

**A little firmer:**
> Hey — I can do today between 4 and 7 near [location]. If you want it, send me a time and I’ll make sure I’m free.

If you want, I can also give you a version that sounds more casual, more professional, or more “selling on Facebook Marketplace.”

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, closer. Still kinda too clean though.

Give me versions that sound like somebody actually posted it and typed fast, not like an example in a guide.

Like:
- 3 title options that feel normal
- photo checklist in plain words
- 2 pickup texts that sound human, especially the firm one

Keep it short.

turns.agent_response:
Got you — more like real person wording.

### Title options
- **iPhone 13 Pro Max 256GB**
- **Samsung S22 unlocked, good shape**
- **Sony headphones, comes with case**

### Photo checklist
- front
- back
- any scratches
- screen on if it has one
- what’s included

### Pickup texts

**Chill:**
> Hey, yeah I’m free today from 4–7 near [location]. If you want to swing by, just let me know.

**A little firmer:**
> Hey, I can do today from 4–7 near [location]. If you want it, send me a time and I’ll make sure I’m there.

If you want, I can make them even more “Facebook Marketplace” casual.

user_simulator.text: Better, but still kind of bland. I wanted it to sound like actual marketplace typing, not just cleaned-up wording with the rough edges shaved off. The firm one still feels a little too polite/fake. Give me something that sounds more like a real person just trying to move it fast.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s the more “typed fast by a real person” version.

### Title options
- **iPhone 13 Pro Max 256GB**
- **Samsung S22 unlocked**
- **Sony headphones w/ case**

### Photo checklist
- front
- back
- any wear
- screen on if it turns on
- what comes with it

### Pickup texts

**Chill:**
> hey im free today 4-7 near [location] if you wanna come by

**More firm:**
> hey im around today 4-7 near [location]. if you want it send a time, otherwise i’m moving on to the next person

If you want, I can do 3 more versions that sound:
- more casual
- more blunt
- more “Facebook Marketplace” unfiltered

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s more like it. Firm one finally sounds like somebody trying to move it, not write a customer service email.
